transcript: LOUD NOISES! Welcome to The Know, I'm Ashley Jenkins. I'm Mika Burton. After months of rumors, Nintendo's been totally silent about whether or not their super popular Pokemon franchise is coming to Nintendo Switch, but now we've finally, maybe, got something a little bit more concrete. And judging by this new information, we might not just have the long-rumored Pokemon Stars coming to Switch, but possibly even a brand new Pokemon game built from the ground up for Nintendo's new console. Okay, I like, I mean, I like this. I'm like. rumored pokemon stars coming to switch but possibly even a brand new pokemon game built from the ground up for nintendo's new console okay i like i mean i like this i'm like i like all of this well it's like i wanted stars or eclipse or whatever calling it but i would take a totally new one uh these details come from two new job postings by japanese developer game freak the outfit responsible for making the mainline pokemon series for nintendo's handheld systems The postings point towards ramped up development for a Pokemon game or games in the multiple even for Nintendo Switch specifically. There are two postings available at Game Freak's website looking for a 3D CG designer as well as a character modeler. While it doesn't outright say that these postings are for Pokemon, there are some pretty blatant hints. For starters, translated posting for the 3d cg designer mentions creating character models in the development of consumer games according to game freak the models will be of deformed toon like characters monsters items and so on and if that's not enough to convince you then what about this little gem we'll have you work on the production production of an RPG game that is popular on a global scale. Gee, a globally popular RPG franchise, a game for Quirks on, whatever could that be? With no clue! They go on to say that it's a title that just about anyone knows, a proposal that could be your future career. But just so you know that they're not in fact simply talking about the regular Pokemon games for the 3ds the posting later mentions the platform will be console additionally game freak also has a posting up for a character modeler looking for someone with experience developing wii u and ps vita level graphics considering game freak typically develops for nintendo although they did develop a game called tembo the badass elephant for xbox one and ps4 back in 2015 and by the way that game is awesome uh but i you know this looks like the rumor pokemon game heading for nintendo switch i don't think anyone's gonna call tembo the badass elephant like popular at a global scale so as we said earlier the idea of a game freak developed pokemon game coming to the switch wouldn't be a total surprise but this would be the first official word we've had of that fact sort of there. There have been other hints as well. There have been rumors. Pokemon company president Tsunekaze Ishihara did confirm to the Wall Street Journal back in September that they'll be supporting the Switch with the Pokemon franchise in some way, but nothing was mentioned at the time about Game Freak specifically. They only popped up in separate rumors. Those rumors concerned Pokemon Stars, which is supposedly a Switch console companion to last year's Pokemon Sun and Moon for the 3DS. Back in November, multiple sources broke the story about Pokemon Stars' development with Game Freak attached as the developer, as it is one to be. At the time, both Eurogamer and Let's Play Video Games' Laura K. Dale wrote about Pokemon Stars, saying the Switch-exclusive title will be an expansion to Pokemon Sun and Moon, similar to Yellow for Red and Blue and Platinum for Diamond and Pearl, and could be seen within a few months of the console's launch. However, Eurogamer's sources indicated it might be pushed back closer to 2017's holiday period. And according to those reports, Starz was already well into the process of being developed as it was developed by Game Freak alongside Sun and Moon. The development teams apparently tag-teamed the work on Sun and Moon and Stars all together, but paused development on Stars late last year so they could polish up Sun and Moon just before launch. YoGamer also said that development started again in earnest once Pokemon Sun and Moon shipped. Now in terms of differences we can expect with the Switch version, YoGamer reported that Pokemon Stars will feature the same map of the Alola region, which isn't too big of a surprise. It will also feature the same art style, but with higher resolution assets. Their sources also claimed you'll be able to trade Pokemon between Stars, Sun, and Moon via Pokemon Bank. And, you know, with Pokemon Bank, you can really trade them from any of the games. But then Nintendo's Switch reveal event came and went in January without any word from Nintendo about when and if we can expect can expect to see pocket monsters gracing their new console the saddest crickets ever however there were a bunch of other tiny things pointing to stars being the real deal like some of the assets and clues that people found buried within sun and moon potentially unrevealed new pokemon for instance along with higher res models way higher than anything needed for the 3ds and the fact that modders have been able to scale the game up to 1080p with really relative ease, which seems to jive with the reports that Sun, Moon, and Stars have been built together with that sort of future-proofing in mind. While Pokemon Stars is rumored to be hitting later this year, and again, it's already supposedly well into its development, there are some tiny indications that this job posting is for a new Pokemon game altogether, one that's not part of the trio of Sun, Moon, and Stars. Another Pokemon Stadium! Yes! No, Pokemon Snap 2. For instance, the contracted period of the 3D CG designer actually runs through May of 2018. Now, if Pokemon Stars is coming this year, there's not really much of a reason to keep stuff on through the first half of next year. Plus, the rumors around Starz only mention maybe around 20 new Pokemon, which doesn't seem like a very tall order for over a year of work. So these details are fueling some speculation that this job posting isn't necessarily about Pokemon Starz, but about a new project we haven't heard before. So Generation 8's possible. It's also entirely possible that we could actually be looking at the Gen 4 remake. Like, Game Freak likes to intersperse their sort of new generations with remakes of previous generations they did it with like X&Y and Auras for example that's just like one of many they seem to be doing that quite a bit so we could be looking at maybe stars here and then a remake and then they do their next generation after that of course there's also the possibility that making Switch exclusive Pokemon games would go against Nintendo's insistence that the Switch isn't meant to replace the 3DS. But who knows, maybe by the end of 2018, it wouldn't feel quite like that was the case. It's also possible that Pokemon Stars isn't quite as far along in the development as everybody made it out to be last year. And both of these job postings are in fact for Stars, which won't be out until until the first half of next year or we're looking at something more along the lines of snap or stadium but those types of titles haven't been handled by game freak in the past but that doesn't mean they can't be now either way it's nice to dream of a future where nintendo brings all of its pokemon games straight to their new console really all especially if they want to move units because we all know that just releasing a Pokemon game will sell millions and millions of Switches. What do you guys think of Game Freak? All but confirming that they are working on a Pokemon game for Nintendo Switch, let us know in the comments. For future updates on all things Pokemon and where to catch them all, remember to like this video and subscribe to The Know. Welcome to The Know, I'm Ashley Jenkins. I'm Mika Burton. After months of rumors, Nintendo's been totally- hey, do I have chocolate right here? Because I just ate a cookie and now I'm really worried about it. Are there cookies? No, you're good. There are cookies, first of all. On the table. Yeah, cookies, cupcakes, like you guys. Thank you. I woke up this morning being like, alright-
